subject: ent-written
title: ENT Written — Exam Notes
description: High-yield written-exam notes — ear, hearing/vestibular, airway/larynx, nose/sinuses, oral cavity, salivary glands, neck — plus an oral-topics recap.

sections.title: Ear — External, Middle, Inner
sections.html:
The external ear includes the pinna and external auditory canal. The middle ear contains the ossicles (malleus, incus, stapes). The inner ear has the cochlea and vestibular apparatus.

Key facts

  • Otosclerosis affects the stapes footplate.
  • Cholesteatoma is a destructive cyst in the middle ear.

sections.title: Hearing & Vestibular Disorders
sections.html:
Hearing loss can be conductive or sensorineural. Vestibular issues include BPPV, Meniere's disease.

Tests

  • Rinne and Weber tests distinguish types of hearing loss.
  • Dix-Hallpike for BPPV.

sections.title: Airway & Larynx
sections.html:
The larynx is responsible for voice production and airway protection. Key structures: vocal cords, epiglottis, thyroid cartilage.

Pathology

  • Laryngeal cancer often presents with hoarseness.
  • Epiglottitis is a medical emergency.

sections.title: Nose & Sinuses
sections.html:
The paranasal sinuses include maxillary, frontal, ethmoid, sphenoid.

Conditions

  • Acute sinusitis usually viral; chronic may need surgery.
  • Nasal polyps associated with asthma and aspirin sensitivity (Samter triad).

sections.title: Oral Cavity
sections.html:
Oral cavity includes tongue, floor of mouth, buccal mucosa, hard palate.

Key

  • Oral cancer risk factors: smoking, alcohol, betel nut.
  • Leukoplakia is a premalignant white patch.

sections.title: Salivary Glands
sections.html:
Major glands: parotid, submandibular, sublingual.

Disorders

  • Sialolithiasis (stones) most common in submandibular.
  • Parotid tumors: pleomorphic adenoma (benign), mucoepidermoid (malignant).

sections.title: Neck Masses & Lymph Nodes
sections.html:
Neck anatomy divided into triangles. Levels I–VI for lymph node classification in cancer staging.

Important

  • Branchial cyst: lateral neck mass in young adults.
  • Thyroglossal duct cyst: midline, moves with swallowing.

sections.title: Oral Topics Recap
sections.html:
Recap of high-yield oral and written topics: cranial nerves, emergency airways, common presentations.

Quick hits

  • CN VII palsy in parotid surgery risk.
  • Tracheostomy vs cricothyroidotomy for emergency.
